title: AWS Multi-Account Baseline — Day-Zero Account Foundation
description: A platform engineer stands up a new AWS account (or a collection of accounts under an Organization) with Hulumi. The target end state: CloudTrail centralised, AWS Config recording, GuardDuty enabled, Security Hub enabled with CIS AWS Foundations v5.0 subscribed, IAM baseline (password policy, no inline admin), and a KMS key ring with automatic rotation. Cross-account topology uses org-delegated services for GuardDuty and Security Hub.
actors: AI Coding Agent (trusted, authoring IaC via Claude Code)
assets: CloudTrail log bucket and KMS key

stride.name: Root credential spoofing
stride.description: An external attacker acquires an AWS root account credential (e.g., via phishing a maintainer) and assumes that identity to reconfigure services out-of-band.

stride.name: CloudTrail tampering
stride.description: A privileged principal (or a compromised IAM role) disables CloudTrail or modifies trail configuration to blind the incident-response pipeline. Prevention: multi-region CloudTrail with log-file validation and CMK-encrypted destination bucket.

stride.name: Repudiation via untagged IaC principal
stride.description: An IaC action appears in CloudTrail but the assumed role does not carry a distinguishing Hulumi tag (`hulumi:iac-role=true`). Without the tag, console break-glass looks identical to IaC deploys, degrading forensic attribution.

stride.name: Information disclosure via misconfigured KMS policies
stride.description: An overly permissive KMS key policy lets a broader principal set decrypt data encrypted at rest. Hulumi's `AccountFoundation` KMS ring (shipping in v0.3) applies `deny-without-tag` policies in Startup-Hardened tier.

stride.name: Denial-of-service via unchecked resource creation
stride.description: A compromised IAM role creates thousands of expensive resources (EC2, NAT gateways, SageMaker endpoints), racking up cost and exhausting service quotas. Mitigations: budget alerts, SCPs restricting resource types, Security Hub findings reviewed promptly.

stride.name: LLM-authored elevation via stale AWS API knowledge
stride.description: Claude Code or another AI agent produces IaC that applies broad IAM permissions (`Resource: *`, `Action: *`) because its training data predates newer least-privilege defaults. Hulumi's baseline components cap these defaults; the `HulumiHardeningPack` (shipping v0.2) blocks wildcard IAM policies at preview time.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.baseline.aws.AccountFoundation
recommendedComponents.rationale: Composes CloudTrail + Config + GuardDuty + Security Hub + IAM baseline + KMS ring with tier-differentiated defaults (Sandbox / Startup-Hardened). Delivers this scenario's target end state as a single ComponentResource invocation. Shipped in M3.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.policies.aws.CisV5Pack
recommendedComponents.rationale: CrossGuard policy pack subscribing to CIS AWS Foundations v5.0.0. Provides preventive policy checks complementing Security Hub's detective findings. Shipped in M3.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.policies.aws.HulumiHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.rationale: Blocks raw `aws.s3.BucketV2`, file:// state backends, and IAM policies with `Resource: '*'` outside `iam:*` reads. The `hulumi:iac-role=true` tag check (advisory in v0.2, mandatory in v1.0.0) is the compensating control for the repudiation threat above. Shipped in M2.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.drift.DriftClassifier
recommendedComponents.rationale: Post-deployment drift triage — distinguishes provider-API churn from console break-glass from genuine IaC drift using four pluggable local adapters. TLA+-verified verdict matrix.
